ranked_movies.title: Airplane!
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively discussed and praised in David Zucker's AMA as a foundational and iconic spoof comedy. Frequently recommended across multiple threads for its rapid-fire jokes and influence on the genre.

ranked_movies.title: The Naked Gun
ranked_movies.reason: Central to David Zucker's AMA and frequently mentioned alongside Airplane! as a pinnacle of spoof comedy. Its legacy and upcoming reboot were key discussion points.

ranked_movies.title: Shaun of the Dead
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended in multiple horror-comedy threads (r/horror, r/MUAEntertainment) for its perfect blend of zombie horror and British humor.

ranked_movies.title: Tucker and Dale vs Evil
ranked_movies.reason: The catalyst for the horror-comedy request thread. Universally recommended as a top-tier example of the subgenre for its clever subversion of tropes.

ranked_movies.title: Cabin in the Woods
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently suggested in horror-comedy threads for its meta-commentary on the genre and its successful mix of scares and laughs. Users emphasized avoiding spoilers.

ranked_movies.title: Rush Hour
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted in the interracial comedy thread as a successful and beloved franchise that exemplifies the buddy-cop, culture-clash formula that is perceived to be missing today.

ranked_movies.title: This Is Spinal Tap
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended in the thread about 'bad' musicians and cited as the quintessential mockumentary. A foundational film in the comedy genre.

ranked_movies.title: Blazing Saddles
ranked_movies.reason: Appeared in multiple recommendation lists for classic comedies. Its edgy, satirical humor was noted as being influential and timeless.

ranked_movies.title: Young Frankenstein
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended in several threads (r/MovieSuggestions, r/MUAEntertainment) as a Mel Brooks classic and a masterclass in spoof comedy.

ranked_movies.title: Harold and Kumar Go to White Castle
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed in the interracial comedy thread as a successful and influential stoner comedy that defined a era of comedy filmmaking.

ranked_movies.title: Cool Runnings
ranked_movies.reason: Cited in the sports comedy thread as a charming, iconic, and emotionally resonant film that is a benchmark for the subgenre.

ranked_movies.title: Major League
ranked_movies.reason: A top recommendation in the sports comedy thread, praised for its humor and beloved characters, often mentioned alongside Caddyshack.

ranked_movies.title: What We Do in the Shadows
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended in both horror-comedy and general comedy threads. The film and TV series were praised for their unique vampire mockumentary style.

ranked_movies.title: Happy Gilmore
ranked_movies.reason: A frequently mentioned favorite in the sports comedy discussion, noted for Adam Sandler's iconic performance and its enduring popularity.

ranked_movies.title: Bhool Bhulaiyaa
ranked_movies.reason: A central example in the Bollywood 2007 discussion, highlighted as a peak comedy hit for Akshay Kumar and a defining film for Vidya Balan.

ranked_movies.title: Return of the Living Dead
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended in horror-comedy threads as a gold standard that perfectly balances comedy and horror, with iconic lines like 'Send more paramedics.'

ranked_movies.title: The Blues Brothers
ranked_movies.reason: Appeared in multiple 80s comedy recommendation lists. Noted for its musical numbers and chaotic energy, fitting the request for R-rated style comedies.

ranked_movies.title: Top Secret!
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed in David Zucker's AMA, with specific questions about Val Kilmer. It is a cult classic from the ZAZ team mentioned alongside their more famous works.

ranked_movies.title: Hera Pheri
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended in the Hindi comedy thread as an iconic and endlessly quotable film that is a benchmark for the genre in Indian cinema.

ranked_movies.title: Walk Hard: The Dewey Cox Story
ranked_movies.reason: The catalyst for the thread about 'bad' musicians. Cited as a brilliant spoof of musical biopics, though the protagonist is ironically talented.
